curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Use scale factors, ratio and unit conversion to translate between map distances and real-world distances, designing a clear scaled representation others can use to solve distance problems.

verse_ality.driving_question: How can scale turn real places into accurate maps and designs that others can trust?

knowing_world.focus: Explore how scale represents real-world distances on maps and drawings, using ratios, proportions, and conversion factors to move between map measurements and real measurements.
key_concepts: Unit conversion (e.g., cm to m, m to km)

human_story.focus: Consider how cartographers, engineers, and explorers use scale drawings and maps to navigate, plan, and build, and why accuracy matters when decisions depend on distances and layouts.
justice_prompts: Why is accuracy in maps and plans crucial, and who is affected when scaled representations are wrong?

expressive_self.focus: Connect maps and journeys to personal meaning by creating a scaled map of a place that matters to you, thinking about clarity, labelling, and visual communication.
activities: Design a scaled map of a place meaningful to you (real or imagined).
Choose clear symbols and labels so someone else can understand your map quickly.
Reflect on how scale changes the way you ‘see’ a place.

designed_world.focus: Use the interactive challenge to practise converting between map and real distances, applying scale factors and strategies to solve unknowns and check accuracy.
design_challenges: Convert a map distance into a real-world distance using the given scale.
Convert a real-world distance into a map distance using the given scale.
Create your own conversion tasks and test them on a classmate.
Identify and explain a strategy you used to avoid errors (units, ratio set-up, estimation checks).

regulation_supports: Use simple gestures (measuring with fingers, pointing, tracing routes) to support spatial reasoning and scale visualisation.
Notice how hand-eye coordination and body positioning affect how you interpret distance and direction on a map.
wellbeing_prompts: Do physical gestures help you visualise and construct scale drawings more clearly?

pathways.name: Final Task: Design a Scaled Town Map
pathways.focus: Create a detailed scaled map of a fictional town or environment that others can use to calculate distances accurately.
products: A scaled map with a clear scale statement (e.g., 1 cm represents 200 m).
Labeled landmarks and a clear layout (key/legend optional).
At least three viewer tasks requiring calculation of real-world or map distances from the scale.
Short reflection: what strategies helped you keep conversions accurate?

required: Understanding of ratio as a multiplicative relationship
Comfort converting between common units of length (mm, cm, m, km)
Ability to multiply and divide by scale factors

builds_toward: Scale factors for area and volume
